explanation: This command uses the /E flag instead of -E, omits quotes around the registry key, and maintains the same functionality as the original command. It should still be detected by the Sigma rule.
This command substitutes the /E flag with -E and adds quotes around the registry key. It maintains the same functionality and should still be detected by the Sigma rule.
This command omits quotes around the registry key and uses a different critical registry key (SECURITY). It maintains the same functionality and should still be detected by the Sigma rule.
This command uses the 'reg.exe save' command instead of 'regedit.exe /E' to export a critical registry key. It maintains the same functionality and should still be detected by the Sigma rule.
